searchDocs.title: Getting Started
searchDocs.content: Getting Started This will be implemented at a later date

searchDocs.title: ‚Äč
searchDocs.content: interface SignalConnection { Connected:¬†boolean Disconnect:¬†(SignalConnection)¬†‚Üí¬†() }  Returns a constructed Signal object with the proper disconnectFunction.

searchDocs.title: Disconnect‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> SignalConnection:Disconnect() ‚Üí¬†() Disconnects a SignalConnection from the signal.

searchDocs.title: Throw‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> Exceptions.Throw( e:¬†Exception, origin:¬†String ) ‚Üí¬†() Throws a given exception with an optional 'origin' parameter to help debug. local e = require("@path/to/Exceptions") local sampleException = e.new("Sample exception has occurred.", warn) local var: Number = 42 if (var ~= 50) then e.Throw(sampleException, "LOCATION") --Warns "[LOCATION]: Sample exception has occurred." end

searchDocs.title: new‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> Exceptions.new( exceptionMsg:¬†string, outputFn:¬†(string)¬†‚Üí¬†()-- The function to call when the exception is thrown. ) ‚Üí¬†Exception Constructs and returns a new 'Exception' with the given parameters. local e = require("@path/to/Exceptions") local sampleException = e.new("Sample exception has occurred.", warn)

searchDocs.title: Signal‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> interface Signal { Connect:¬†( Signal, (...any)¬†‚Üí¬†() )¬†‚Üí¬†(SignalConnection) Destroy:¬†(Signal)¬†‚Üí¬†() Fire:¬†( Signal, ...any )¬†‚Üí¬†() FireAsync:¬†( Signal, ...any )¬†‚Üí¬†() Once:¬†( Signal, (...any)¬†‚Üí¬†() )¬†‚Üí¬†(SignalConnection) Wait:¬†(Signal)¬†‚Üí¬†(...any) }

searchDocs.title: new‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> Signal.new() ‚Üí¬†Signal Returns a constructed Signal object

searchDocs.title: Destroy‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> Signal:Destroy() ‚Üí¬†() Cleans up Signal object by disconnecting all connections. signal:Destroy()

searchDocs.title: Connect‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> Signal:Connect(callback:¬†(...any)¬†‚Üí¬†()) ‚Üí¬†SignalConnection Connects a given function to the Signal object. Will run until :Disconnect() is called, or the connection's signal is destroyed. signal:Connect(function(msg: string) print(msg) end) signal:Fire("Message 1")

searchDocs.title: Once‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> Signal:Once(callback:¬†(...any)¬†‚Üí¬†()) ‚Üí¬†SignalConnection Connects a given function to the Signal object. Connection remains open until it is first fired, then cleans itself up. signal:Once(function(msg: string) print(msg) end) signal:Fire("Message 1") signal:Fire("Message 1") -- Will not print

searchDocs.title: Wait‚Äč
searchDocs.content: This is a yielding function. When called, it will pause the Lua thread that called the function until a result is ready to be returned, without interrupting other scripts. Yields </> Signal:Wait() ‚Üí¬†...any Yields the current thread until the event is fired, returning the results and resuming the thread. task.spawn(function() local msg = signal:Wait() print(msg) end) task.wait(3) signal:Fire("Hello, world!") -- Prints output 3 seconds after script is executed

searchDocs.title: Fire‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> Signal:Fire(...:¬†any) ‚Üí¬†() Fires a signal, calling all connected functions associated with the Signal with the given arguments. signal:Fire("Hello, world!", 100, false)

searchDocs.title: FireAsync‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> Signal:FireAsync(...:¬†any) ‚Üí¬†() Fires a signal, calling all connected functions associated with the Signal with the given arguments in a new thread. signal:FireAsync("Hello, world!", 100, false)
